nativeRange.habitat: Hybrid cultivar with no wild population. Commonly cited as a Heinz Kasselmann selection from the late 1980s involving Echinodorus barthii x E. horemanii 'Red' parentage. Parent genus Echinodorus is generally native to wetlands of the tropical Americas.

decorPreferences: Deep nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or capped sand) — heavy root feeder
Iron-rich root tabs near the root zone are generally considered the key driver of the ruby-red coloration
Open background space with at least 45 cm of vertical clearance
Moderate to bright lighting tends to deepen the red coloration
Moderate flow; avoid placement directly in jet returns
Plant the crown just above the substrate surface — buried crowns are prone to rot

summary: The Red Rubin Sword is a large hybrid Echinodorus cultivar prized for its ruby-red new growth, which matures to a deep red-green. A popular background plant for medium-to-large planted tanks, it is generally regarded as one of the easier swords to keep, with iron supplementation widely considered the key driver of its signature color.
careNotes:
The Red Rubin Sword (Echinodorus sp. 'Red Rubin') is a hybrid cultivar with no wild population, commonly cited as a selection made by Heinz Kasselmann in the late 1980s from parentage typically reported as Echinodorus barthii crossed with E. horemanii 'Red', though the exact lineage varies by source. It circulates in the trade under several names, including Echinodorus 'Rubin' and Red Rubin Amazon Sword. Adult height generally runs 25 to 60 cm when fully submerged, placing it among the larger common sword plants.

Care follows the standard Echinodorus pattern: the Red Rubin Sword is a heavy root feeder that tends to perform best in a deep, nutrient-rich substrate such as aquasoil, capped sand with root tabs, or laterite. The crown (the central growing point from which new leaves emerge) should be planted just above the substrate surface, as buried crowns tend to rot. Iron-rich root tabs placed near the root zone are widely regarded as the single most important factor in maintaining the ruby-red coloration; without iron supplementation, new growth has been reported to emerge in a more olive-green tone. Moderate to bright lighting tends to deepen the color further. CO2 injection is not required but has been reported to improve leaf size, density, and color intensity. Recommended water parameters run from 20 to 28 C and pH 5.5 to 8.0, with broad tolerance of hardness — generally the same envelope as others in the Echinodorus genus.

As a background plant, the Red Rubin Sword generally wants at least 45 cm of vertical clearance and an open footprint of 30 cm or more. Propagation is typically by adventitious plantlets (small daughter plants) that form on flower stalks or side shoots, and by crown division. The species has been reported to be sensitive to copper-based medications, so any fish treatment containing copper should be avoided in tanks where it is grown.

Sister entries: fw-plant-011 (Amazon Sword), fw-plant-127 (Ozelot), fw-plant-128 (Rose), fw-plant-142 (Kleiner Bar), fw-plant-150 (Melon), fw-plant-157 (Marble Queen Radican), fw-plant-158 (Red Flame).

primary.name: Modern Aquarium - Red Rubin Echinodorus product/care page
primary.notes: Confirmed rosette growth form, heavy root feeder, moderate light, easy care, runner/plantlet propagation, temp range 68-82 F (20-28 C), iron supplementation for color.

additional.name: Buce Plant - Echinodorus 'Red Rubin' product page
additional.notes: Cross-confirmed family Alismataceae, height 25-61 cm, pH 5.5-8.0, moderate light, heavy root feeder, fast growth, background placement, adventitious-shoot propagation. Also flagged copper sensitivity.
